The comment that I see the most frequently is I wish we had a teacher like this growing up. The first thing I have to mention is her accent and the melody that exists in her voice. What? It's so unique. Chances are you've never heard anybody sound like her before. Even though she has an accent you are still fully able to understand her because she articulates extremely well. Accents are never a problem. Articulation is. She has incredible use of volume. All right. Beautiful facial expressions. Extremely expressive and then explosive body language which is what keeps her not only auditorily so engaging. You see when most people teach they think the only way to teach others is through lecturing. We've all had that boring lecturer and why was that lecturer boring? Because they just lectured. The difference with this particular teacher here is that she used the props to demonstrate. When you lecture people just hear what you're saying. When you demonstrate people hear what you're saying, they see what you're saying, they feel what you're saying, they learn and they remember. Do you think these kids are going to forget this teacher?
